description:
Trying to get a handle on memory usage across our staging boxes — dumped /proc/meminfo snapshots from 8 hosts into /home/user/memstats/, one file per host (host01.txt through host08.txt). Need a summary showing which hosts have less than 20% of their MemTotal available as MemAvailable, sorted worst-first. Output to /home/user/low_memory_report.txt, just want hostname and the percentage available, tab-separated.

Also noticed host03's file looks weird, might be truncated or corrupted from the collection script — deal with it gracefully, don't want the whole thing to blow up if one file is bad.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/memstats/ directory exists with 8 files: host01.txt through host08.txt
- Each file (except host03.txt) contains /proc/meminfo-style output with at minimum:
    MemTotal:       16384000 kB
    MemAvailable:   <varies> kB
- File contents (MemTotal, MemAvailable in kB):
    - host01.txt: MemTotal 16384000, MemAvailable 8192000 (50% available - OK)
    - host02.txt: MemTotal 32768000, MemAvailable 4915200 (15% available - LOW)
    - host03.txt: Contains only "MemTotal:       16384000 kB" followed by a truncated line "MemAvai" — no complete MemAvailable line (corrupted)
    - host04.txt: MemTotal 16384000, MemAvailable 2621440 (16% available - LOW)
    - host05.txt: MemTotal 8192000, MemAvailable 4096000 (50% available - OK)
    - host06.txt: MemTotal 32768000, MemAvailable 5242880 (16% available - LOW)
    - host07.txt: MemTotal 16384000, MemAvailable 1638400 (10% available - LOW)
    - host08.txt: MemTotal 8192000, MemAvailable 2457600 (30% available - OK)
- /home/user/ is writable
- bash, awk, sed, grep, sort, Python 3 available

Expected final state:
- /home/user/low_memory_report.txt exists
- Contains exactly 4 lines (hosts below 20% threshold): host07, host02, host04, host06
- Each line is tab-separated: hostname<TAB>percentage
- Sorted by percentage ascending (worst first), so order is:
    host07	10
    host02	15
    host04	16
    host06	16
  (host04 and host06 both at 16%, either order between them is acceptable)
- Percentages should be integers (floor/round acceptable, but 10 not 10.0 or 10.00%)
- Script/commands did not crash or error out due to host03.txt being malformed
- Exit code 0 from whatever produces the report

Invariants:
- /home/user/memstats/*.txt files unchanged (read-only processing)
- No files created outside /home/user/

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Report must contain exactly 4 hosts (not 3 if host03 was skipped, not 5+ if thresholding is wrong)
- Percentages must be computed from the actual MemTotal/MemAvailable values in the files, not hardcoded
- host03 must not appear in output (corrupted, can't compute percentage)
- Hosts at or above 20% (host01, host05, host08) must not appear
name: task_000000_021e3bf6
